name: Lamb Steaks with Vermouth and Rosemary
recipeIngredient: 6 Lamb steaks from the boned rib OR loin, trimmed into round steaks 1 inch thick
1/2 teaspoon chopped dried rosemary or 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Rub lamb with a mixture of garlic, salt, pepper, and rosemary.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over moderate heat.
4. Sauté meat slices until well browned on one side. Pour off excess fat from the pan.
5. Turn and brown the other side. Remove and keep warm in an ovenproof serving dish.
6. Add the remaining tablespoon of butter to the same skillet and heat.
7. Add onion, shallot, lemon peel, and mushrooms. Sauté until softened.
8. Add vermouth and pour over meat. Cover the serving dish loosely with aluminum foil.
9. Bake for about 10 minutes for pink lamb or 15 minutes for well done.
10. Remove foil and bake an additional 5 minutes.
11. Season to taste and sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving. Serves 6.
